key_insight: Using reverse Fisher divergence instead of forward Fisher divergence makes gradient descent globally convergent for Gaussian-mixture score matching under stronger, more favorable optimization dynamics.
engineering_relevance: This suggests a more stable training objective for generative modeling, diffusion-style score estimation, and unnormalized density fitting when mixture structure is present.
method: Optimize reverse Fisher divergence, where expectations are taken over the student distribution.
Prove global GD convergence for a Gaussian teacher with a Gaussian-mixture student having fixed weights and identity covariances.
Extend to Gaussian-mixture teachers under random initialization and a ~Ω(1)-separation condition on target means using a Lyapunov-based dynamics analysis.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports theoretical convergence guarantees, including high-probability component matching and total-variation convergence under stated separation and initialization assumptions.
watch_item: The guarantees rely on fixed weights, identity covariances, random initialization, and sufficiently separated target means, so performance may degrade outside this regime.
paper_title: Global Convergence of Gradient Descent for Score Matching in Gaussian Mixtures via Reverse Fisher Divergence
